payload.text: an easy to understand music distribution pltform. https://www.landr.com/pricing/release automates the distribution of creators songs. links them to unique idetifyer on near network. pays industry highest ryalties. we need to be alternatives spotify so its also a music and video streaming platform decentralized. only get demonetizedif the majority vote. part of the music machine node for zero latency real timecpo creating . from idea to to fan base zeo friction. frictionfree.? possible name

**Conceptualizing Decentralization**

I'm currently focused on the decentralized nature of the platform. I'm exploring how blockchain technology can ensure fair compensation for artists and transparent royalty distribution. I'm also considering the implications of user-owned content and governance within the system.


**Analyzing System Architecture**

I've been drilling down into the system architecture, prioritizing automated royalty distribution and blockchain integration with NEAR for unique identifiers. The goal is to build something visually stunning and incredibly functional. Zero-latency co-creation with the "Music Machine Node" is a key priority. I'm exploring the "Idea to Fanbase" concept to eliminate friction. I'm focusing on features such as decentralized moderation and streaming.


**Developing Minimal Viable Product**

I'm now narrowing down the scope to build a functional MVP for the prompt, encompassing a compelling landing page, a creator dashboard with upload functionality and simulated analytics, a listener streaming interface with a mock decentralized voting system, and a demonstration of the "Music Machine Node" concept to foster real-time co-creation.  I'm also working on core file structure, including `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and React components such as `Header.tsx` and `Hero.tsx`.


**Constructing Components Systematically**

I'm now building out key components in a methodical way. The "Spotify alternative," `StreamPlayer.tsx`, and the decentralized moderation system, `VotingSystem.tsx`, are taking shape. I'm focusing on the integration of Gemini to analyze lyrics and generate metadata for simulated minting on NEAR. I'm also preparing to simulate the process of linking a Unique Identifier. I'm aiming for a dark mode, neon-accented, Web3 aesthetic. The Co-Pilot interface will simulate zero-latency co-creation.


**Formulating Execution Strategy**

I'm solidifying the implementation steps. I'm prioritizing the `metadata.json` permissions for the microphone and defining types for `Track`, `Vote`, and `CoCreationSession`. I'm also preparing to develop the Gemini service functions, including `analyzeTrackMetadata` and `generateLyrics`. I will then construct key components such as `Layout`, `Player`, and `Dashboard`, ensuring tight integration in `App.tsx`. I'm starting to write code, refining icon use and styling with Tailwind.


**Integrating Google AI**

I'm now integrating the Google Gemini SDK for metadata generation using `gemini-2.5-flash`. I'm simulating a voting system with a "demonetization" warning after 50% downvotes. I'm focusing on subtle NEAR branding and a royalty comparison chart using Recharts, plus an audio visualizer.


**Organizing Core Files**

I'm establishing a streamlined code structure, focusing on key components. I'm building a reusable UI via `components/Button.tsx` and adding a visualizer via `components/Visualizer.tsx`.  I'm splitting `App.tsx` into views: `Dashboard.tsx`, `Studio.tsx`, and `Streaming.tsx`. The aim is a modular design for improved readability and maintainability. This structure sets the stage for the platform, tentatively named "SonicNode".

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind and Font Awesome.
Service for AI features using Gemini 2.5 Flash.
Main application component containing routing logic and sub-views.
